channel.title: Luke Alex Davis
channel.description: The website of Luke Alex Davis.

item.title: 1 vs 10,000
item.description: Today, I realised that I'm trying to compete with teams of writers.

item.title: 1 Year Later
item.description: It's been a year since the death of George Floyd. But what has changed?

item.title: Inspiring Creatives For 2017
item.description: The following creatives have helped me in the past and they're worth keeping an eye out for in 2017.

item.title: 1001 Movies You Must See Before You Die (2008 edition)
item.description: I decided to see how many films I'd seen from the 2008 edition of 1001 Movies You Must See Before You Die

item.title: 100th Blog Post
item.description: This is technically the 100th blog post on this iteration of my blog.

item.title: The 16 games I completed in 2025
item.description: The most games I've ever played and completed in a single year and it was a blast

item.title: 2017 Needs Brevity And Nuance
item.description: This post has been on my mind for a while and seeing as it's the start of a new year, I thought it necessary to write it now while I'm in a writing mood.

item.title: 2024 needs patience and assertiveness (for me)
item.description: This post has been on my mind for a while and seeing as the new year is approaching, I thought I'd write my thoughts down.

item.title: 24 Hour Virus
item.description: For the first time in a year, I got sick.
It was my 32nd birthday on Monday. Here's how I spent it.

item.title: 4 Creative Article Ideas - Inspired By "The Read"
item.description: Here are 4 creative article ideas, inspired by The Read

item.title: 69 simple pleasures
item.description: I'm a simple man with a NICE! number of pleasure.

item.title: A note on some notes on writing
item.description: It's very meta but I like to write about writing.

item.title: A poem about how search engines can't interpret poems
item.description: The following is a poem about how search engines can't interpret poems using GPT-3.5 Turbo

item.title: Adapt or die? Why only two options?
item.description: Yes, I'm back again with an AI Opinion. I wish I didn't have so many but people keep saying nonsense things.

item.title: AI in SEO makes me anxious
item.description: Neither a source of promise nor happiness in those models

item.title: AI is a lot like an orange
item.description: There are plenty of analogies for AI but mine is the best.

item.title: AI as a tool to lower the inertia of getting started
item.description: AI isn't the thing. It's the thing that gets you to the thing.

item.title: AI should be a last resort
item.description: We're told that AI is simultaneously the present and the future and you'll be a husk of a human being if you don't just embrace it and get on the train. Here's why that's untrue.

item.title: Am I helping or harming?
item.description: Every day, tech makes the world a little—or a lot—worse. And I wonder how much I'm contributing to that.

item.title: Another week in Lisbon
item.description: From 18th–23rd November, I spent another week in Lisbon with my partner. Here's how it went.

item.title: My Albums Of The Year List: 2016
item.description: Here's my list of the best albums of 2016

item.title: My Albums Of The Year List: 2021
item.description: Here's my list of the best albums of 2021

item.title: My Albums Of The Year List: 2022
item.description: Here's my list of the best albums of 2022

item.title: My Albums of the Year: 2023
item.description: Here's my list of the best albums of 2023

item.title: API vs. chatbots? They're singing from the same hymn sheet
item.description: They have their pros and cons but they're not directly opposed. The key difference is manual fine-tuning.

item.title: Back on Twitter
item.description: I'm back on Twitter. For now.

item.title: Bad Boys For Years
item.description: A short list of films called 'Bad Boys' that don't feature Will Smith or Martin Lawrence.

item.title: Beached
item.description: A really cool tilt-shift video featuring people on the beach.

item.title: Being Black in digital marketing in 2023
item.description: In a follow-up to my 2020 piece ‘Being Black in Digital Marketing’, I asked the same Black marketers if anything has changed in the last 3 years.

item.title: Beyond tired
item.description: Some thoughts on being mentally and physically tired.

item.title: Beyond tired: Part 3
item.description: Some thoughts on my mental health during the pandemic.

item.title: Beyond tired: Part 4
item.description: Some thoughts on racism in football and in general

item.title: Beyond tired: Part 5
item.description: Some thoughts on my mental health and therapy.

item.title: Beyond tired: Part 6
item.description: Some thoughts on my mental health and burnout

item.title: Beyond tired: Part 2
item.description: Some thoughts on racism and 'at least they did something'.

item.title: Black and Tired
item.description: How many times are non-Black people gonna derail the Black Lives Matter hashtag and in how many different ways?

item.title: Black Panther and therapy
item.description: I went to therapy last year and talked about Black Panther.

item.title: What is blackness?
item.description: Is it a closed question? Can it be worn like a winter coat or a pair of cool sunglasses? Does it give owners freedom of their cities?

item.title: Blogging as self-expression
item.description: For me, blogging is about self-expression (alongside self-experimentation).

item.title: Blogging gratitude
item.description: I'm very protective over the art of blogging because, without it, I would absolutely not be where I am today. No way.

item.title: Blogs and hats
item.description: I've noticed something about how I approach blogging.

item.title: Boats, storms, and rockets
item.description: Are we really 'in this together'?

item.title: BOOM! Tetris for Jeff
item.description: This video gives me unbridled joy and Jeff Moore is arguably my favourite gamer ever. He's such a cool guy and an awesome Tetris player.

item.title: Brief LinkedIn thoughts on AI
item.description: I wrote a comment on a LinkedIn post about AI and what it means in SEO.

item.title: Ceci n’est pas une blog
item.description: The title says “This is not a blog” and plays on Rene Magritte's The Treachery of Images. It is also true.

item.title: Chronological ordering of posts is good actually
item.description: People organise things differently but there's no single best way in my opinion.

item.title: Connections
item.description: Some thoughts about how connections in life.

item.title: Eff COVID-19
item.description: Some angry thoughts on COVID-19.

item.title: Deleting Twitter
item.description: I'm seriously considering doing it because I've had enough of the place.

item.title: Don't delete your old work (but restore it if you do)
item.description: Why you shouldn't delete your old work and find value beyond the monetary.

item.title: Don't overcomplicate your projects
item.description: Perfect is the enemy of done.

item.title: Eight
item.description: I finally got a new job so I'm celebrating with words.

item.title: I'm an Emptygold Blogger
item.description: I started yet another blog.

item.title: Everyone's a writer
item.description: As long as you have intent, it doesn't matter whether your scribbles are intelligible or not—you are a writer

item.title: Failure
item.description: Some thoughts on failure and why it means different things for different people.

item.title: My Favourite Albums of 2020
item.description: A list of my favourite albums from 2020.

item.title: Feeling Guilty For Pursuing Your Passions
item.description: I used to feel really empowered and inspired when I worked on my music or blogged until one day I was questioned about it.

item.title: 8 things I learnt in my 5th year as a professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 5 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 7 things I've picked up over that period.

item.title: 19 Things I Learnt In My 1st Year As A Professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for a year now. But what have I learnt? I wrote a list of 19 things I''ve picked up over that period.

item.title: 8 Things I Learnt In My 4th Year As A Professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 4 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 8 things I've picked up over that period.

item.title: Future Coca-Cola slogans with GPT-3.5
item.description: Some weird and wonderful(?) taglines generated by a large language model

item.title: Future job: Web historian
item.description: If money was no object, I'd love to become a Web history expert for the period between 1989-2003(ish).

item.title: Gains
item.description: Today was the first day I got back into exercise after a brief illness.

item.title: I bought a GameSir G8 Galileo and a Peladn WI-6 mini PC
item.description: Or, why I didn't spend £300 on a Switch OLED

item.title: Generative AI bores me to tears and that's a privileged stance
item.description: I can't know how to hear anymore about chatbots.

item.title: Get a website
item.description: The title says it all: get yourself a website.

item.title: GitHub as a CMS?
item.description: Sometimes, the second best solution is right in front of you.

item.title: "Bad faith" AI critiques need better understanding
item.description: The negativity makes sense. But maybe we should unpick it and learn why it's there in the first place.

item.title: Halt and Make Fire
item.description: Over the past week, I've been studying early internet culture (inspired by Halt and Catch Fire) and it's really made me think about how SEO is structured now compared to back then.

item.title: Have you heard of codeswitching?
item.description: Still reading through kottke.org's archives and found a 2003 post on 'bilingual conversations'

item.title: Hayfever and Histamines
item.description: Some thoughts and facts on hay fever and histamines

item.title: Headless chickens
item.description: Some thoughts on people's alarming responses to the COVID-19 pandemic

item.title: How Astro changed my life on the Web
item.description: Or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love JavaScript.

item.title: How I display my Last.fm top 1000 artists
item.description: A lengthy post on how I create my list of Last.fm top 1000 artists

item.title: How much do my blogs cost?
item.description: Blogging costs money to maintain. Here's how much.

item.title: I also love my website
item.description: My website is a labour of love. But it's mine.

item.title: I am...
item.description: All the things that I am.

item.title: I am not a content creator
item.description: It's funny what comes into your head while you're washing up.

item.title: I don't use all the features in Ableton and that's okay
item.description: To whomsthever needs to hear it, you're doing great sweetie x

item.title: I've started a linkblog
item.description: Sometimes, you want to have a place to have lots of cool links without it being hosted by a third party. That's what a linkblog is all about.

item.title: If Nobody Listened, Would I Still Make Music?
item.description: Or: why I make music?

item.title: I'm Racially Tired
item.description: Some thoughts on my racial tiredness.

item.title: Inspiring creatives for 2025
item.description: The following creatives have helped me in the past and they're worth keeping an eye out for in 2017.

item.title: Introducing RALTS (Really Awesome Lexicon and Tag Suggester)
item.description: I love NLP and taxonomies. Here's a script I wrote that marries the two concepts.

item.title: Is SEO on shaky ground?
item.description: The more that things change, the more that they become disruptive (not a compliment).

item.title: It's not computer science!
item.description: Still super wild that I dropped out of a computer science degree when I was 18 and now I'm using machine learning, language models, and reading papers on this DAILY.

item.title: Give The Drummer Sum: J Dilla’s Influence on the Modern Drummer
item.description: J Dilla was mean on the MPC but his live drumming had influences on a number of his peers who have since helped carry his legacy on.

item.title: Review: Jamiroquai – Rock Dust Light Star
item.description: What I really like about this album is that it fills in any gaps made from the other Jamiroquai releases.

item.title: Kamala Harris is still a cop
item.description: Why are people acting like she's the first Black person to be part of an election campaign or run for office or whatever?

item.title: Keep writing anyway
item.description: Writing is a marathon, not a sprint, or a less tired analogy.

item.title: Khruangbin & Federer
item.description: Two of my favourite things in the world: a great band and a great athlete.

item.title: Last Donut of the Night: when Federer & Dilla united for a weekend
item.description: Tennis and hip hop rarely come together (bar Drake and Redfoo’s attempts.) But this is completely different. To tell this story, I need to break it down into three parts.

item.title: My Last.fm Playback 2025
item.description: Now that Spotify is over and cancelled, I decided to check out my Last.fm stats for 2025

item.title: My Letterboxd Year In Review for 2022
item.description: I'm not a Letterboxd Pro user so I only get the basic year review stats but here they are.

item.title: My Letterboxd Year In Review for 2023
item.description: Clue's in the title: this is my basic Letterboxd year in review.

item.title: My Letterboxd Year In Review for 2024
item.description: Clue's in the title: this is my basic Letterboxd year in review for 2024

item.title: My Letterboxd Year In Review for 2025
item.description: Clue's in the title: this is my basic Letterboxd year in review for 2025

item.title: Some links on tech
item.description: I keep accumulating links to send to work so I thought I'd put them here instead of clogging up online chats.

item.title: My media of the year: 2024
item.description: Since everyone is doing end of year lists, I thought I'd do one too but for movies, games, books, and music.

item.title: My media of the year: 2025
item.description: I forgot that I did this for 2024 so here's my media of the year for 2025!

item.title: Meta-meta-blogging
item.description: Is there a word for soul searching but for blogs? Meta-meta-blogging will have to do for now.

item.title: Music and me
item.description: A reminder to whomever needs it that you can just make something without overthinking it

item.title: My 49 Favourite Albums of 2012
item.description: A list of my 49 favourite albums of 2012

item.title: My Album of the Year List: 2014
item.description: A list of my fave albums from 2014

item.title: My big Pokémon self-project
item.description: After watching the Pokémon World Championships last week, I felt inspired to get all the legendaries from every game up to SV (Scarlet & Violet).

item.title: My Albums of the Year: 2011
item.description: A list of my fave albums from 2011

item.title: My Five Favourite Video Games of All Time
item.description: A list of my five fave games of all time

item.title: My Idea Debt
item.description: Earlier this week, I read about a concept called Idea Debt. It’s when you hold onto ideas without actually putting them into place.

item.title: My love of Sony hardware design
item.description: I've not owned many Sony products but I've always admired their design. Here are some of my favourites.

item.title: My New Obsession Is... Sega
item.description: I thought I’d revive this series where I talk about my latest obsession. My latest craze is Sega.

item.title: My Phone History
item.description: I've had 13 phones to date. Here they all are.

item.title: My Record Store Day
item.description: As a producer and crate digger, Record Store Day is a like a second Christmas.

item.title: My Review of 2017
item.description: This was going to be a lot more dreary and I wasn't going to publish anything.

item.title: My Spotify Unwrapped: 2024
item.description: I don't normally write a blog post for my Spotify Wrapped but what the hell.

item.title: My Week In Lisbon
item.description: From 20th - 27th November, I spent a week in Lisbon. It was a life changing experience.

item.title: New normal
item.description: 'The phrase ‘new normal’ is one that grinds my gears in a very particular way, because look around… nothing has fundamentally changed.'

item.title: Night off
item.description: Tonight, I deactivated from Twitter. It's only for the night although if tomorrow is as bad as today, I'll extend it.

item.title: There's no reason to 'embrace' AI in SEO
item.description: Artificial intelligence? Yeah, that sounds about right.

item.title: Not sure about this AI productivity thing
item.description: If it's meant to be so much easier, why are there so many iterations without a quality guarantee?

item.title: Visualising @nyt_said_first
item.description: The people behind @nyt_first_said created a visual 'machine' of how they find the unique words they tweet.

item.title: Open Offices Are Overrated
item.description: Vox Media have made a video all about open offices as part of their Overrated series.

item.title: Post-It Note directories
item.description: Web directories are long out of fashion but the purpose of them has never died - informational retrieval and discoverability.

item.title: Use Prince's purple for a:visited in your CSS
item.description: A way to add some purple to your web design

item.title: Probably Autistic
item.description: My name is Luke and I'm Probably Autistic.

item.title: Prompt engineering
item.description: I still feel weird about how prompt engineering is promoted as a new concept.

item.title: Proust Questionnaire
item.description: I answered the Proust Questionnaire. Here are my answers.

item.title: Proust Questionnaire - 2020
item.description: In 2012, I answered the Proust Questionnaire and brought it over from my old blog. I'm taking it again.

item.title: Public health
item.description: Imagine public health policies that included mental + physical check ups with dedicated healthcare professionals, accomodating for different cultures, genders, sexualities, + disabilities etc. etc.

item.title: Questions about my blog
item.description: A Q&A about my blog that nobody asked for.

item.title: I refactored my website and I hated doing it
item.description: Web design is my passion but only when it works the first time.

item.title: RIP MJ - 8 Years On
item.description: I can't believe it's been 9 years since Michael Jackson died.

item.title: The Numbers Game: Roger Federer
item.description: My vote for the male GOAT and the numbers that make him so.

item.title: 12 Things I Learnt In My 2nd Year As A Professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 2 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 12 things I've picked up over that period.

item.title: The SEO of Pokémon nicknames
item.description: Something I've been doing recently is looking for Pokémon nickname ideas. They have some cool SEO behind them.

item.title: SEOicism
item.description: If you're overwhelmed by all the SEO tips, future trends, Python scripts, automation ideas, folks convinced tech SEO is a waste of time and it's all about CONTENT and LINKS, or the folks obsessed with talking about dinosaurs for some reason... so am I.

item.title: 6 things I learnt in my 7th year as a professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 7 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 6 things I've picked up in that time.

item.title: Short form blogs are subversive
item.description: I'm making up excuses to justify my shorter blogs while everyone else writes long form articles.

item.title: I redesigned my site a little
item.description: You may have noticed the site looks a little different. Here's why I did made some design changes.

item.title: 5 things I learnt in my 6th year as a professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 6 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 5 things I've picked up over that period.

item.title: Slow connection speeds are an everybody problem
item.description: No matter where you are in the world, you could end up with dial-up-like speeds and it be out of your control.

item.title: Social anxiety
item.description: How my social anxiety has grown since COVID-19

item.title: Some brief thoughts on robots.txt
item.description: I wrote some thoughts about robots.txt and how you should use it.

item.title: Some thoughts on search engines
item.description: I wrote some thoughts about search and why we can't build a better search engine than Google without eclipsing its power and magnitude.

item.title: Some brief thoughts about web devs and web performance
item.description: I hope this doesn't get me fired or cancelled but this needs to be said.

item.title: Some caveats to SEO and web performance
item.description: As a technical SEO, I am very passionate about web performance. Here's why nuance is needed when talking about its relationship with SEO

item.title: Some (genuine) thoughts on vibe coding
item.description: Why does this new terminology make me cringe and yet I've felt compelled to use it?

item.title: Some thoughts on Seirdy's takeaways from the Google content doc leak
item.description: Sorry, I couldn't think of a more SEO title for this, heh heh.

item.title: Somebody* on life learning
item.description: But what is Maya Angelou?

item.title: Sometimes, automation is slower
item.description: For when copy and paste, Regex, and fast fingers are quicker than Python

item.title: I'm not really one for speeches so I'll just say 'thanks'
item.description: Some thoughts on the BLM protests and blogging and stuff.

item.title: Still Elephant
item.description: An anecdote from 2004 about Columbine

item.title: Sustained grief
item.description: Some thoughts on sustained grief, inspired by bell hooks

item.title: We talk, work, and think different thanks to the Web
item.description: How the Web has affected our behaviour

item.title: The Colour Of Pi
item.description: Thanks to a professor from San Diego University, we now have the colour of pi and other mathematical constants.

item.title: The Exclusive Website
item.description: The Exclusive Website is an exclusive club in the form of a website with cheesy elevator music in the background (which you can mute).

item.title: The Last 5 Games of the 2017 Australian Open
item.description: Or: the greatest sporting moment in my life.

item.title: The Luke 100
item.description: It's a list of 100 things I want to do in my life.

item.title: The meaning of life
item.description: Today, I had a taxipiphany an epiphany in a taxi on my way home from my son's school.

item.title: The Problem With Google It
item.description: You never know, by answering the question, you could learn something yourself - I know I have

item.title: They don't see us as human
item.description: Some thoughts on the othering of marginalised groups

item.title: Things that AI isn't
item.description: A short list of what AI isn't

item.title: Things to remember for 2023
item.description: What we need to keep in mind this year.

item.title: 12 Things I Learnt In My 3rd Year As A Professional SEO
item.description: I've been a SEO for 3 years now. But what more have I learnt? I wrote a list of 12 things I've picked up over that period.

item.description: Content creation flow? That's so 2020.

item.description: Here is how I create content

item.description: I thought that I'd throw my two pence in regarding blogging and loneliness.

item.description: Some thoughts in response to overzealousness.

item.title: Today is a special day
item.description: 29th January is special for two reasons.

item.title: Trolls and pills
item.description: Taken from a 2006 NYT article called How to Be a Curmudgeon on the Internet

item.title: How To Use Twitter Lists To Reduce Your Following Count
item.description: Whether you use Twitter for work, personal branding, or escapism, who you follow plays an integral part.

item.title: Am I underthinking RSS?
item.description: There's a lot of talk about a new RSS reader and I'm wondering what all the fuss is about

item.title: Unusual side effect
item.description: Who gets an urge to buy a Windows Phone? Must be something in this vaccine...

item.title: I've updated my website again
item.description: I can never settle.

item.title: Vibe coping
item.description: Just how I like to get through the day.

item.title: I tried Vicuna and it made me laugh
item.description: Some thoughts on the hilarity of large language models

item.title: Videos I've been watching to keep me going
item.description: Videos that makes me laugh and give me needed serotonin

item.title: We are not data points. We are people.
item.description: If being Black, queer, and neurodivergent has taught me anything, it's that you don't need data and reports to tell you to treat people with love and compassion.

item.title: We Need To Use The Lang Attribute More
item.description: Why I love different languages and I admire polyglots.

item.title: Weather-beaten
item.description: Burnout has been on the tip of our tongues for god knows how long. But today, I wondered if that was the right word to describe how I've been feeling lately and for a while.

item.title: Web fonts aren't the devil
item.description: You don't need holy water; just a surgical mindset when it comes to Web performance and aesthetics.

item.title: What are your most used apps?
item.description: My most used apps in 2021

item.title: What's the obsession with folding phone?
item.description: I don't get the obsession with folding phones. At all.

item.title: Why do rich people make poor decisions?
item.description: Rich people get away with murder and get praised for it.

item.title: Yet another week in Lisbon
item.description: I spent 5 days in Lisbon from 21st–26th November. Here's how it went.

item.title: You don't have to learn Python to work here but it might help
item.description: Python. It's a word that either strikes fear into the hearts of SEOs or ignites a fire in their bellies. I can tell you: I’ve felt both.

item.title: You rocked my world, you know you did…
item.description: My relationship with rock music has been… well, rocky, for lack of a better word.

item.description: Another video games list, but more about me this time.

item.description: I like numbers.

item.description: Garbage in, garbage out and back in again

item.description: Sometimes the best inspiration comes from the people you love

item.title: Black survival is Black excellence
item.description: The title says it all.

item.title: blogging is whatever you want it to be
item.description: no more definitions, no more guidelines. just write.

item.description: The title says it all.

item.title: Cherish
item.description: Whoever or whatever you love, cherish them.

item.description: a poem by me about autumn days as a kid

item.title: don't let Web nostalgia obscure a positive Web future
item.description: It's not 1995 anymore and that's okay!

item.description: A list of lyrics I love.

item.title: Happy accident #1
item.description: My son wanted to listen to something on Spotify. What happened next will SHOCK you.

item.description: A mini step-by-step process of how I produce music

item.title: I want this digital radio
item.description: Yes, the Revo SuperSystem is sold out on the offical website but it looks so good.

item.description: Things I'd want if I was rich.

item.description: A poem about my time.

item.title: -lh-

item.title: LLM evangelism
item.description: I had some unfiltered thoughts about LLM evangelists and their practices and now you all have to read to them.

item.title: My style guide
item.description: This is my WIP style guide for all sites that I own.

item.title: Things that aren't in the U.S. Constitution
item.description: I get sick of hearing about 'freedom of speech', especially in the UK who have, like a lot of things, adopted it from the US because they have no ideas of their own

item.title: Personal lexicon
item.description: A list of words I use in my life that are unique to me.

item.title: Reasons to get Ableton Live Standard edition
item.description: If you’re wondering why I'm putting this on the internet, I don’t understand the question and I won’t respond to it.

item.title: Saddam Hussein had a Bacon number of 3
item.description: Saddam Hussein was CONNECTED

item.title: SAVER STORE
item.description: A haiku about one of my favourite shops growing up.

item.title: search works best when everyone involved is honest and helpful
item.description: if this social contract breaks down, people at the bottom suffer the most

item.title: Só cansado
item.description: Nobody's gonna read it.

item.title: Sound familiar?
item.description: Some thoughts on people's cookie cutter conversations.

item.title: the near infinity of pixels
item.description: the vastness of the universe is right in front of us in tiny square form

item.title: Visionary
item.description: Data > Experience > Opinion > Vision

item.title: Wander in Porto
item.description: I've never been to Porto but after extolling the virtues of Lisbon, people have recommended it to me. Maybe one day.

item.title: what if personal sites were like service stations or truck stops?
item.description: places to stop off and take a rest from Social Media™

item.title: the web is built on the backs of weird web shit
item.description: make what you want and how you want but have fun

item.title: Where There's Gold
item.description: I'd forgotten just how great this song by Seal was.

item.title: Some brief thoughts on generative AI
Do as I do because I said so
